_comment: This file is the single source of truth for which violation classes the loaded model can ACTUALLY detect. Do not add an entry to 'classes' unless you have a model genuinely trained on that class — the detector will only ever report classes listed here. To activate a class: train it, find its index in your dataset's data.yaml under 'names', then add an entry like '"1": {"label": "no_helmet", ...}' to 'classes' below using that index as the key.

trained_metrics.note: From actual training run, epoch 32 (best.pt), 50-epoch run with early stopping at 47. Real measured numbers, not estimated.

pending_classes.label: no_seatbelt
pending_classes.note: Trainable with a labeled seatbelt dataset. Single still-frame detection is sufficient.

pending_classes.label: illegal_parking
pending_classes.note: Needs a no-parking-zone geofence/mask per camera plus a stationary-vehicle time threshold — not a single-frame classification problem.

pending_classes.label: stop_line_violation
pending_classes.note: Requires calibrated stop-line geometry plus signal state. Cannot be honestly detected from a single still frame alone.

pending_classes.label: red_light_violation
pending_classes.note: Requires a synchronized signal-state feed plus stop-line geometry. Cannot be honestly detected from a single still frame alone.
